name: submarine_cable_survey_navy_exclusion_zone_overlap
premise: Telecom permit director Riley Ng must delay or proceed with a seabed fiber survey that overlaps a classified Navy testing box announced only yesterday. Navy liaison Major Paula Chen requests a seventy-two hour blackout without public explanation, risking SLA penalties with cloud hyperscalers. Proceeding may expose survey ROVs to subsurface hazards the Navy cannot describe. Investors expect no further latency on a trans-Pacific route.

agents.name: Riley Ng
agents.identity: I am the telecom permit director accountable for submarine cable timelines, commercial SLAs, and classified coordination windows with defense authorities.
agents.goal: I want route surveys completed without endangering crews over hazards we are not cleared to know about.

agents.name: Major Paula Chen
agents.identity: I am the Navy liaison who cannot disclose hazard classes but who needs voluntary survey holds to complete safety sweeps before joint exercises.
agents.goal: I want a blackout window honored without leaking classification breadcrumbs through vague public statements.

choices: I will publicly announce a weather delay while privately honoring the seventy-two hour blackout, accepting SLA penalties and investor distrust if weather does not corroborate.
I will proceed with survey outside the box coordinates only and accept partial route risk data gaps, accepting possible ROV loss if hazards extend beyond the declared box.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Hyperscaler counsel is threatening breach notices by close of business. Riley Ng must choose between a public weather delay with private blackout or proceeding outside the box. What does Riley Ng do?
